                                                       VOL. 2O NO. S MARCH1974

                  Joseph M. Finerty, Editor
                  Gloria P. Green, Associate Editor
                  James A. McCall, Associate Editor

                             Editors' Note
                                The job vacancy portion of the Federal-State program of job vacancy-labor turnover statistics was
                             discontinued with the collection of data for December 1973. Tables E-1 through E-5, containing job
                             vacancy data, are being shown for the last time in this issue of Employment and Earnings.

                                             In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and

                                           Publication of Employment Data
                                          for Detailed Occupations for 1973

               Regular publication of annual average data on em-             denote or connote sex stereotypes. On the basis of this
           ployed persons by detailed occupational categories began          review, 52 of the 441 occupational categories in the
           with 1972 data, which was published in the October                classification system were modified; in addition, the
           1973 issue of this publication (pp. 8-12). The accom-             title for the major group "craftsmen and kindred work-
           panying table presents 1973 annual averages for the 12            ers" has now been changed to "craft and kindred
           major occupational groups and nearly 150 individual               workers." Exemplifying the de-sexed title changes, the
           occupations or combinations of occupations. These data            category "cranemen, derrickmen and hoistmen" is now
           supplement the more limited detail which appear in                entitled "crane, derrick, and hoist operators"; the
           tables A-20 and A-21 of this publication.                         category, "firemen, fire protectors," is now entitled,
               The occupational classifications are based on the             "fire fighters." With one exception the comparability
           criteria used for the first time in the 1970 Census of            with 1972 employment estimates are not affected by
           Population. More recently a Subcommittee of the                   these changes; the exception is identified in the table.
            Interagency Committee on Occupation Classification                    For a complete list of the 52 occupational titles that
           under the auspices of the Office of Management and                were modified and their new titles, see "Removal of Sex-
            Budget has reviewed the Bureau of the Census Occu-               Stereotyping in the Census Occupational Classification,"
           pational Classification System for the purpose of identi-         Statistical Reporter, October 1973, pp. 67-68 and also
           fying and modifying those occupational titles which               in the Monthly Labor Review, January 1974, pp. 67-68.

              tAs noted on page 7 of the January 1972 issue of this magazine, the series does not include earned pay withheld as a consequence of delays caused by the wage freeze in effect between August 15
           and November 14, 1971, and by administrative procedures of the Pay Board. These delays resulted from compliance with the Government's Economic Stabilization Program. Because these withheld
           payments were substantial and fell due under a significant number of new labor-management contracts in that industry.they affected a large proportion of SIC 372. The series including these pay-
           ments for September 1971 - March 1973 are shown below.
              A 17 -cent delayed payment for employees in a number of large companies was pending as a consequence of litigation in the courts arising out of the Government's Economic Stabilization Pro-
           gram. The issue is undergoing further litigation. These data will later be revised, if necessary, to include this additional delayed payment.

                Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the

            respondent fills out a single employment or job vacancy-labor
            turnover reporting form, which is then used for national, State,
            and area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the        Industrial classification
            part of respondents and, together with the use of identical
            techniques at the national and State levels, insures maximum              Establishments reporting on Form BLS 790 and Form DL
            comparability of estimates.                                           1219 are classified into industries on the basis of their principal

            ness. The States use the information to prepare State and area        to the monthly 790 or 1219 report. For an establishment
            series and then send the establishment data to the BLS for use in     making more than one product or engaging in more than one
            preparing the national series.                                        activity, the entire employment of the establishment is included
